refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure teleoperation end-to-end communication latency p95 ≤ 150 ms under agreed network conditions to enable safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is decomposed to explicitly state the latency metric and its impact on safe teleoperation, focusing on measurable performance.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure end-to-end latency over the network link under defined conditions and verify p95 latency ≤ 150 ms during teleoperation sessions.
sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency in the network link to support teleoperation.
FR2: Ensure timely processing and forwarding of teleoperation commands and telemetry within the NGHE solution.
FR3: Provide real-time telemetry and tasking interfaces with latency guarantees.

candidate_dps: DP1: Use optimized network protocols and QoS prioritization for teleoperation data.
DP2: Implement edge computing within the NGHE solution to reduce processing delays.
DP3: Design dedicated teleoperation communication channels with latency monitoring.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Use optimized network protocols and QoS prioritization for teleoperation data.

potential_sources: Tight coupling between network protocol optimization and teleoperation command processing.
coupling_check.notes: The DP logically addresses the FR with minimal cross-domain coupling; network protocol optimization is a focused solution.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions are stable and within agreed parameters.
Risk of latency spikes due to unforeseen network congestion.
Assumes NGHE solution can enforce QoS policies end-to-end.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider adding edge computing (DP2) if network optimization alone does not meet latency requirements.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for teleoperation command processing, telemetry handling, and network communication optimization.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface for sending and receiving teleoperation commands and telemetry data with latency constraints.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Provides real-time telemetry data to other modules and stakeholders.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives teleoperation task commands from higher-level control modules.

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad scope but less focused on teleoperation-specific latency optimization.
Primarily physical vehicle control, less suitable for network protocol optimization.
Highest containment score; directly responsible for teleoperation communication and latency.
Focuses on safety alerts, not communication latency.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning the DP to the Teleoperation module centralizes latency-sensitive communication logic, minimizing diffusion and improving maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on network link performance outside module control.
Risk of interface overload if Teleoperation module handles too many responsibilities.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Monitor interface complexity and consider splitting Teleoperation module if diffusion risk increases.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (network protocol optimization and QoS) directly addresses the refined FR with clear latency targets and measurable verification.

diffusion_risks: Potential overloading of Teleoperation module with multiple communication responsibilities.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module shows strong containment for the DP with minimal diffusion, supporting clear ownership.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Latency can be measured end-to-end via telemetry and network link interfaces, enabling verification of FR within the assigned IM.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear mapping from FR to DP to IM with well-defined interfaces and ownership.

recommended_revisions: Evaluate network link stability assumptions in more detail.
Consider contingency DPs if latency targets are not met under real conditions.

decision.justification: The functional decomposition level is complete with clear FR refinement, DP selection, and IM assignment meeting all criteria and passing gate reviews.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency in the network link to support teleoperation.
FR2: Ensure timely processing and forwarding of teleoperation commands and telemetry within the NGHE solution.
FR3: Provide real-time telemetry and tasking interfaces with latency guarantees.

revise.why: Current level decomposition is sufficient; next steps involve decomposing sub-FRs if needed.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Teleoperation module can enforce QoS and protocol optimizations.
open_risks: Latency spikes due to network congestion.
evidence_needed: Latency performance data under operational network conditions.
